dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8417 IH: Keeping China Off the Rails Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moolenaar (for himself and Mr. Gottheimer) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To amend title 49, United States Code, to require all railroad freight cars operating on the United States general railroad system of transportation to meet certain manufacturing and content requirements, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Keeping China Off the Rails Act.

section.header: Amendments to requirements for railroad freight cars placed into service in the United States
section.text: Section 20171 of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1) by striking A railroad freight car wholly manufactured on or after the date that is 1 year after the date of issuance of the regulations required under subsection (c)(1) and inserting A railroad freight car described in paragraph (3);
in paragraph (2) by striking described in paragraph (1) in each place it occurs and insert described in paragraph (3); and
by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a railroad freight car described in this paragraph is—

subparagraph.text: for the 1-year period beginning on the date of enactment of the Keeping China Off the Rails Act, a railroad freight car produced during the 2-year period immediately prior to such date of enactment;
for the 1-year period beginning 1 year after such date of enactment, a railroad freight car produced during the 5-year period immediately prior to such date of enactment;
for the 1-year period beginning 2 years after such date of enactment, a railroad freight car produced during the 10-year period immediately prior to such date of enactment;
for the 1-year period beginning 3 years after such date of enactment, a railroad freight car produced during the 15-year period immediately prior to such date of enactment; and
on and after the date that is 4 years after such date of enactment, a railroad freight car.

paragraph.text: in subsection (c)(3)(B) by striking Association of American Railroad’s Umler system and inserting Association of American Railroads’ Umler system.
